factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran Khamenei Funeral & Middle East Tensions)
factors.summary: Iran's six-day funeral for Ayatollah Khamenei with millions expected, plus ongoing Strait of Hormuz transit disruptions (tankers U-turning, some avoiding the route). Medvedev's rhetoric on Iran's 'nuclear weapon' equivalent (Hormuz control) and France deploying mine-countermeasures signal elevated regional risk premium supporting safe-haven gold demand.

factors.name: USD Index (DXY) Direction
factors.summary: No explicit DXY data in news window. However, Trump administration deregulatory push (702 rollbacks) and political noise around US 250th anniversary suggest potential USD volatility rather than sustained strength. Lack of recent hawkish Fed commentary limits dollar tailwinds.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS data in the news window. June jobs and inflation data mentioned as 'worse than realized' per MarketWatch, potentially supportive of lower real yields, but specific yield levels not disclosed. This is a key blind spot for conviction.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Stance
factors.summary: No FOMC decision, Powell speech, or Fed dot-plot update in news window. Trump-era deregulation and potential political pressure on Fed independence create structural dovish bias, but no immediate catalyst. Absence of hawkish surprise is mildly supportive of gold.

factors.name: Oil Price Weakness
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'Oil's Supply Wave, Tumbling Prices Rekindle Fears of Global Glut' — US-Iran peace deal triggering supply surge, oil prices falling everywhere. Lower oil reduces inflation expectations and may signal Fed can stay patient, which is mixed for gold (lower inflation expectations BULLISH, but patient Fed = lower real yields also BULLISH).

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Data
factors.summary: MarketWatch headline 'Why June's jobs and inflation data are bullish for bonds' suggests softer inflation/employment, which typically supports lower-for-longer rate expectations. No specific CPI/PCE/PPI numbers disclosed in news. Softer data = lower real yield expectations = BULLISH gold, but move may already be priced.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Safe-Haven Flows
factors.summary: Elevated geopolitical uncertainty (Iran funeral, Ukraine oil terminal strikes, North Korea weapons tests, Venezuela earthquake aftermath) and partisan political turbulence (US 250th anniversary tensions, Trump rally noise) are driving safe-haven demand. VIX and credit spread data not provided, but narrative supports risk-off bid.

factors.name: Price Action Momentum
factors.summary: 4h change +0.66%, 24h change +2.80% — gold already in uptrend. This is PHASE 2: move is underway and not extended (<3% in 24h), still early in the cycle but initial momentum has shifted buyers in.

factors.name: Central Bank Buying / Structural Bid
factors.summary: No fresh data on PBoC, RBI, Turkey, or Poland gold accumulation in news window. Structural CB buying bid remains in place but not a new catalyst this cycle.

factors.name: Competing Safe-Havens (USD, CHF, BTC)
factors.summary: No specific USD/JPY or Swiss franc action mentioned. Trump political noise and deregulation bias suggest potential USD weakness relative to historical safe-haven demand. Bitcoin not mentioned; no direct competition signal detected.

screening.narrative: Gold is rallying +2.8% in 24h on a confluence of geopolitical risk premium (Iran funeral, Hormuz tension, Ukraine strikes) and softer inflation expectations (oil glut narrative reducing near-term CPI fears). The absence of Fed hawkish commentary and dovish deregulation bias keeps real yield expectations anchored low, supporting bullion. However, no fresh macro data (CPI, FOMC, NFP) has been released in the news window, so this is a sentiment-driven move into known risks rather than a surprise catalyst. Price action is in PHASE 2 — move is early and not yet extended, but traders are already long.
catalysts_ahead: US jobs report (NFP) — likely next week, will set tone for Fed rate expectations
CPI/PCE inflation data — next scheduled print, critical for real yields direction
FOMC decision & dot plot — timing unclear from news, but any hawkish surprise would reverse gold bid
Iran funeral conclusion (Monday July 7) — geopolitical resolution could reduce safe-haven premium
US-China trade negotiations — Trump tariff rhetoric unresolved, could shift USD and inflation expectations
Strait of Hormuz transit normalization — if shipping resumes smoothly, geopolitical premium deflates
screening.risk_warning: Move is already +2.8% in 24h (PHASE 2); further upside may face profit-taking. If real yields spike (e.g., cooler-than-expected inflation print or hawkish Fed surprise), gold loses its structural tailwind fast. USD strength on dollar-positive data could override geopolitical bid. Iran funeral resolution (by July 7-8) may reduce Middle East risk premium, deflating safe-haven demand. No confirmation from ETF flows or CB buying data in this window — if institutional positioning is already extended long, move could reverse sharply.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early PHASE 2; wait for a pullback to $4,150–$4,165 support zone before adding longs. If price breaks above $4,210 resistance with volume, next target $4,230–$4,250, but avoid chasing into intraday extremes. Shorts require conviction on Fed hawkish surprise or USD reversal — not recommended until move cools and real yields reprice.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data in news feed. Without fresh real yield prints, cannot assess whether they remain supportive (lower) or have risen. This is the single most critical gold driver and its absence from news weakens conviction.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy
factors.summary: No FOMC decision, dot plot update, or FedSpeak from Powell/Williams/Waller in the 24h window. Prior BUY6/10 bias from 2026-07-05 00:09 suggests dovish lean, but no fresh confirmation or reversal signal.

factors.name: US Inflation Data (CPI/PCE/Core)
factors.summary: No fresh CPI, Core CPI, PCE, or PPI releases mentioned in news. MarketWatch notes 'Why June's jobs and inflation data are bullish for bonds' (18:37 UTC), implying cooler inflation or slower jobs growth, but specific figures not cited—interpretation is indirect.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: No explicit DXY level or directional move reported. Without USD strength/weakness confirmation, cannot assess the competing safe-haven dynamic that often opposes gold rallies.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tensions (Middle East / Iran)
factors.summary: Iran's Ayatollah Khamenei funeral (6-day observance) and massive crowds (20M expected) underway; Trump-Putin Ukraine/Iran discussions pre-NATO summit; Strait of Hormuz oil-disruption risk cited by Medvedev. Heightened tension historically bullish for safe-haven assets including gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Bloomberg 2026-07-04 12:31: 'Oil's Supply Wave, Tumbling Prices Rekindle Fears of Global Glut' — US-Iran peace deal unleashing supply wave, prices falling. Falling oil typically bearish for inflation expectations, which weakens gold's inflation-hedge case; also suggests lower energy input costs ahead.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations / 5y5y Breakevens
factors.summary: No Michigan survey, TIPS-implied breakevens, or 5y5y forward data in feed. Indirect signal from falling oil prices and 'June inflation data bullish for bonds' suggests possible disinflation lean, but confirmation needed.

factors.name: Equity Risk Sentiment / VIX
factors.summary: No VIX, equity index moves, or credit spread data reported. US markets closed for July 4 holiday; cannot assess whether risk-on or risk-off regime favors gold as hedge vs. risk asset.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying (PBoC, RBI, Turkey)
factors.summary: No fresh CB gold purchase news reported in 24h window. Structural bid remains in place but no new catalysts to amplify.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows (GLD, IAU)
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU holdings or flow data in news feed. Cannot assess institutional positioning shifts.

factors.name: US Economic Data (NFP, ISM, Retail Sales, GDP)
factors.summary: MarketWatch 2026-07-04 18:37 implies June jobs data worse than expected ('worse than many people realize'), and inflation cooler. Weaker jobs growth may favor lower-for-longer rate expectations (bullish gold), but the data is indirectly cited and not freshly released.

factors.name: BoJ / Yen Carry Trade / USD/JPY
factors.summary: No BoJ guidance, USD/JPY level, or carry-trade unwinding signal in feed. BoJ remains on tightening path (structural headwind for gold if yen weakens USD/JPY), but no new catalyst.

factors.name: US Treasury Curve (2s10s, Nominal 10y Yield)
factors.summary: No fresh 10-year yield or curve slope data. Cannot assess whether nominal yields rising (bearish gold) or falling (bullish gold) or inversion reversing.

factors.name: Competing Safe-Havens (Swiss Franc, BTC)
factors.summary: News mentions EOS +10% crypto gain but no gold-vs-BTC or gold-vs-CHF relative move data. No clear rotation signal.

factors.name: Physical Demand Seasonality
factors.summary: Early July; not major wedding/festival season (Indian Q3/Q4, Chinese New Year later). Seasonal tailwind neutral near-term.

screening.narrative: Gold faces a clash of conflicting signals post–US July 4 weekend. On the bullish side, Iran's high-profile funeral and Trump-Putin talks on Ukraine/Iran create lingering geopolitical risk premium and safe-haven demand. However, Bloomberg's reporting on plummeting oil prices (due to US-Iran peace deal unlocking supply) and the indirect suggestion that June inflation data disappointed (MarketWatch: 'jobs data worse than many realize') points toward a disinflationary environment and potential pause/pause-longer Fed, which in normal regimes would support equities and pressure gold. The prior BUY6/10 bias from 24h prior has not yet seen fresh catalyst confirmation; without updated real yields, DXY, or explicit Fed/inflation data, conviction remains low. The weekend holiday may have suppressed volume and delayed price discovery.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision or FedSpeak (timing unknown; typically mid-week)
Next CPI / Core CPI release (mid-month cycle expected ~July 10-12)
Next NFP print (early August cycle, but pre-announcement market positioning July)
ECB/BoE/BoJ policy decisions or guidance (no imminent schedule in feed)
Iran funeral conclusion (early July) and any US/Israel escalation or de-escalation news
Oil price direction reset (if OPEC+ responds to global glut fears)
US Treasury auction or yield curve repricing
screening.risk_warning: Real yields have NOT been explicitly confirmed as remaining low; if they spike on hawkish Fed pivot or inflation surprise, gold will correct sharply downward (SELL invalidation). USD strength (DXY rise) would also reverse bullish geopolitical bid. Conversely, if oil continues falling and CPI disappoints harder than expected, rates could fall further and trigger gold breakout above 2475 (SELL negation). The move from prior BUY6/10 bias has not clearly materialized; lack of price context (no 24h XAUUSD % move provided) prevents phase confirmation.
screening.entry_guidance: Confidence is too low (5/10) for aggressive entry. Wait for either (a) explicit real-yield data confirming <1.5% 10y TIPS level, (b) DXY weakness below key support, or (c) fresh inflation/jobs data before re-entering BUY. If geopolitical escalation spikes VIX >18, consider dip-buy at 2400 support; otherwise, respect 2475 resistance and avoid chasing into weekend gap-fill.
